response: The internet originated in the 1960s with U.S. Department of Defense-funded ARPANET, which was demonstrated in 1969, and grew to connect universities and research institutions in the 1970s; the 1983 transition to TCP/IP protocols established the foundation for the modern internet, followed by Tim Berners-Lee's 1989 invention of the World Wide Web (with HTML, URLs, and HTTP) and 1993's Mosaic browser, which made the web accessible to ordinary users. The late 1990s dot-com boom saw massive investment in internet companies, many of which failed in the 2001 crash, though survivors like Amazon and Google became some of the most valuable companies; the mid-2000s brought social media (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube) and, after 2007's iPhone, mobile internet, while 2006's AWS launched cloud computing as a major force. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly every aspect of modern life, from commerce to communication to entertainment.
